evidence_excerpt: Despite inotropic support... his clinical condition continued to worsen and he died on fourth postoperative day.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: He continued to have low-cardiac output state with gradual worsening of urine output and renal function; treated for presumed sepsis.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: The child continued to have heart failure and failure to thrive, and therefore early surgical repair was contemplated.
Postoperatively, the child required high inotropic support and could not be weaned off the ventilator.
Despite inotropic support, the child continued to have low-cardiac output state with gradual worsening of urine output and renal function... treated for presumed sepsis.
He died on fourth postoperative day.

notes_on_uncertainty: Long-term quality of life cannot be assessed beyond the immediate postoperative period because the patient died shortly after surgery; functional and symptom assessments reflect the perioperative course after diagnosis.
